Note: Each keyword you specify, opens in a new tab. 

It is simple, productive and efficient!
Advanced tab and session manager with multi-window support, session switching, and window restoration

Drowning in tabs? Take back control of your browser.

  Smart Session Management
  Create unlimited sessions within each browser window. Switch instantly between work, research, and personal browsing without losing a single tab. Your tab groups, colors, and organization stay exactly how you left them.

  AI-Powered Summaries
  Never forget what you were working on. Our built-in AI automatically summarizes each session based on your browsing activity—so you can jump back into any project with full context, even days later.

  Multi-Window Mastery
  Managing multiple windows? We've got you covered. See all your windows at a glance, track tab counts, and seamlessly switch between them from one elegant interface.

  Never Lose Your Work Again
  Accidentally closed a window? Restore your last 10 closed windows with all sessions intact. One click brings everything back.

  Privacy First
  All data stays on your device. AI processing runs locally via Chrome's Gemini Nano—no cloud services, no tracking, no account required.

  Key Features:
  - Unlimited sessions per window
  - One-click session switching
  - AI session summaries (powered by Gemini Nano)
  - Closed window restoration
  - Tab group preservation
  - Memory-efficient tab suspension
  - Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+Shift+F / Cmd+Shift+F)

  Stop juggling tabs. Start managing them.
One-click grouping, smart auto-reorder, multi-window support, instant search, custom thresholds, exclusions and dark theme.

TabChaos v1 by Wafflemind.com

TabChaos v1 delivers effortless, intelligent browser tab management for casual and power users alike. Its clean interface and advanced features keep your workspace organised, efficient and focused.

Key Benefits and Features:
– Auto Group/Ungroup: Instantly group all open tabs by domain—or revert them to individual tabs—using a single click or customizable keyboard shortcut.
– Cross-Window Grouping: Manage related tabs across multiple browser windows as one unified group, eliminating clutter and streamlining your workflow.
– Configurable Thresholds: Define the minimum number of tabs required before grouping activates, so you decide when and how your tabs are organised.
– Smart Reorganisation: Whenever you open a new tab, TabChaos automatically reorganises your groups to keep related content together.
– Quick Search: Find any tab in seconds—filter by title or URL with the built-in search bar.
– Ignore List: Add specific domains to an exclusion list so they always remain as standalone tabs.
– Dark Mode: Reduce eye strain and enhance focus in low-light environments with a sleek dark-theme option.

TabChaos v1 transforms chaotic tab overload into a streamlined, efficient browsing experience. Download it today at tabchaos.wafflemind.com and take control of your tabs.

Tmux Tab Groups brings powerful, keyboard-first navigation to your browser tab groups. Inspired by the popular terminal multiplexer tmux, this extension lets you manage tabs using familiar, efficient keyboard shortcuts.

KEY FEATURES:

• Keyboard-First Navigation - Control tabs without reaching for your mouse
• Tmux-Inspired Shortcuts - Use Ctrl+A prefix commands for intuitive control
• Quick Tab Switching - Jump to any tab with number keys (1-9, 0)
• Tab Group Management - Create, organize, and manage groups effortlessly
• Hibernate Groups - Save tab groups as bookmarks for later restoration
• Fuzzy Finder - Quickly search and jump to any group
• Session Persistence - Restore your workspace exactly as you left it

BASIC COMMANDS:

All commands start with Ctrl+A, followed by:
• n/p - Next/Previous tab
• l - Last (previously active) tab
• 1-9, 0 - Jump to specific tab in current group
• c - Create new tab in current group
• g - Create new tab group
• a - Add last tab to current group
• x - Hibernate current group (save to bookmarks)
• f - Open fuzzy finder for quick navigation
• ? - Show help window with all shortcuts

WHO IS THIS FOR?

Perfect for developers, power users, and anyone who values efficiency and keyboard navigation. If you've ever used tmux or prefer keeping your hands on the keyboard, this extension will transform how you manage browser tabs.

PRIVACY & OPEN SOURCE:

This extension does not collect or transmit any data. All tab information stays local on your device. The code is fully open source and available on GitHub.

Get started by pressing Ctrl+A and then ? to see all available shortcuts!
Vertical tab groups dock with glassmorphism. Hover the left edge to reveal your tab groups as a sleek macOS-style sidebar.

VertiGlass brings macOS dock elegance to your Chrome tabs.

✨ FEATURES
• Glassmorphism design — frosted glass blur with depth and transparency
• Hover-to-reveal dock on the left edge of any page
• Tab groups displayed as compact icons
• Tree expansion — click a group to see all tabs branch out
• First tab's favicon represents each group
• One-click tab switching and closing
• Buttery smooth animations

🎯 HOW IT WORKS
1. Hover your mouse near the left edge of any webpage
2. The glass dock slides in with your tab groups
3. Click any group icon to expand its tabs in a tree
4. Click a tab to switch, or hover to close

🔒 PRIVACY
• Zero data collection
• No external servers
• Works 100% locally
• Minimal permissions (only tabs & tabGroups)

Perfect for power users, tab hoarders, and anyone who appreciates beautiful, functional design.
Lightning-fast universal launcher. Press ⌘. to instantly search tabs, bookmarks, history, or the web.

Focus Dock: The Ultimate Universal Search & Productivity Launcher

Stop digging through hundreds of tabs. Focus Dock is the fastest Universal Search and Tab Manager for Chrome. Press ⌘+. (Cmd+Period) from any webpage to instantly launch your command center. No signup, no configuration—just pure productivity.

Why Focus Dock is the #1 Productivity Launcher: Our "Zero-Setup" architecture means you are productive the second you install. Whether you need a bookmark manager, a tab search tool, or a quick notes app, Focus Dock handles it all through a single, keyboard-first interface.

🚀 Core Features (100% Free): 

• Instant Universal Search: Find open tabs, bookmarks, and browser history with lightning-fast fuzzy matching (even with typos). 
• Tab Management & Groups: Use /groups to organize your workspace or /autogroup to sort tabs by domain instantly. 
• Quick Notes: Type /note to capture ideas without leaving your current page. • Reading List: Save any article with ⌘+Shift+S and access it later with /read. 
• Workspaces: Save and restore entire tab sessions for different projects using /save. 
• Clipboard History: Access your recently copied links and text using /clip.

⚡ Smart Commands for Power Users: Focus Dock turns your browser into a command line. 
Navigate like a pro with 100% keyboard support: 

• /github — Instantly see all open GitHub tabs. 
• /workspaces — Switch between project sessions. 
• /index — Rebuild your local search index for real-time results.

🔒 Privacy-First Design: 

• Everything Stays Local: Your history, notes, and search data never leave your browser. 
• No Accounts Required: Start using all core features immediately without a login. 
• No Tracking: We believe your browsing data belongs to you.

Free vs. Pro (Flagship Access): Focus Dock is free to use forever with all core features included. Upgrade to Pro for unlimited workspaces, unlimited notes, 100 clipboard history items, and visual tab thumbnails.

Perfect for: 

• Developers managing complex documentation and dozens of tabs. 
• Researchers building a Second Brain and gathering resources. 
• Students switching between multiple project workspaces. 
• Power Users who want to work at the speed of thought.

Stop wasting time switching tabs. Experience the power of a universal browser launcher. Install Focus Dock and press ⌘+. now.
An open source Chrome extension that extracts page context, assembles a prompt, and runs it in Claude.

Precog extracts structured context from the page you're on and assembles a Claude prompt from modular instruction blocks.

Press Cmd+Shift+P (Mac) or Ctrl+Shift+P (Windows/Linux) on any webpage to open a command palette. Precog reads the current page and presents a set of instruction blocks: summarize, identify TODOs, draft a reply, create an Asana task, search for related context across Gmail and Slack, or do deep research. Blocks can be combined. Precog assembles the page context and selected instructions into a prompt and sends it to Claude.

Example: open Precog on a Gmail thread and select the following blocks:

- Search for deep context: Claude searches Gmail, Google Drive, and Slack for related threads, documents, and messages, so the response is informed by the full history rather than just the current email.
- Identify TODOs: extracts TODOs from the thread. In "advise" mode, it also suggests approaches with confidence levels.
- Draft a reply: generates a response based on the thread content and any context found in the search.
- Create Asana task: creates a task to track follow-up, with a title, description, and link back to the email.

Precog has specific integrations for Gmail, Asana, and Slack. On Gmail it reads the full thread with subjects, senders, and dates. On Asana it pulls the task title and description with formatting and links preserved. On Slack it gets the message text, sender, channel, timestamp, and permalink. On any other page, Precog captures the page title, URL, and any selected text or page content.

For best results, enable connectors in Claude for Gmail, Google Drive, Slack, Asana, and any other services relevant to the context being processed.

Precog runs entirely in the browser. There is no server, no analytics, and no tracking. Data is only sent to Claude when a prompt is explicitly submitted.

Open source (Apache 2.0): https://precog.cc
Intelligently organize your browser tabs with AI-powered grouping and smart suggestions.

Tired of tab chaos? Meet Smart Tab Organizer - your intelligent browser companion that transforms tab management from a chore into a breeze!

🧠 AI-Powered Organization
Smart Tab Organizer uses advanced machine learning to automatically group your tabs by topic, project, or purpose. No more hunting through dozens of tabs - everything is organized logically and instantly.

⚡ Lightning-Fast Performance
Built with cutting-edge technology, our extension works seamlessly in the background without slowing down your browser. Experience smooth, responsive tab management that keeps up with your workflow.

�� Smart Suggestions
Get intelligent recommendations for tab organization based on your browsing patterns. The more you use it, the smarter it gets - learning your preferences to provide personalized organization.

🔒 Privacy-First Design
Your data stays on your device. We don't collect, store, or transmit your browsing information. Your privacy is our priority.

✨ Key Features:
• Automatic Tab Grouping - AI-powered organization by topic and context
• Smart Suggestions - Personalized recommendations for optimal organization
• One-Click Organization - Instantly organize all tabs with a single click
• Custom Grouping - Create and manage your own tab groups
• Keyboard Shortcuts - Quick access with customizable hotkeys
• Clean Interface - Intuitive design that doesn't clutter your browser
• Performance Optimized - Lightweight and fast, no browser slowdown

🎨 Beautiful & Intuitive
Enjoy a clean, modern interface that integrates seamlessly with Chrome. Our thoughtfully designed UI makes tab organization feel natural and effortless.

🚀 Perfect For:
• Professionals - Keep work and personal tabs organized
• Researchers - Group related research tabs efficiently
• Students - Organize study materials and resources
• Developers - Separate project tabs and documentation
• Anyone - Who wants a cleaner, more organized browsing experience

💡 How It Works:
1. Install the extension
2. Browse normally - Smart Tab Organizer works in the background
3. Organize with one click when you're ready
4. Enjoy a cleaner, more productive browsing experience

Transform your browsing experience today with Smart Tab Organizer - where intelligence meets simplicity!

Note: This extension requires access to your tabs to provide intelligent organization. For enhanced AI-powered grouping, it may request access to your browsing history to learn your patterns. Important: The extension only READS your history for analysis - it never modifies, deletes, or changes your browsing history in any way. All processing happens locally on your device to ensure your privacy and security. The extension works perfectly even if you choose not to grant history access.
Sidebar extension for managing tabs in a tree structure

もう、タブの迷子にならない。
Chromeのサイドパネルを利用して、増えすぎたタブを「ツリー形式」や「最近使った順」でスマートに整理・管理できる究極のタブマネージャーです。

【魅力的な詳細説明】
「調べ物をしていたら、いつの間にかタブが細くなって中身が分からない…」
「さっきまで見ていたタブがどこにあるか見つからない」
そんなストレスを、Tree Tab Managerが解消します。

Google Chromeの最新機能（Side Panel API / Manifest V3）をフル活用し、ブラウジングを妨げない快適な操作感を実現しました。

【主な特徴】
🌿 視覚的に整理する「ツリー表示」
リンクから開いたタブを自動的に「親子関係」として階層表示。情報の関連性が一目でわかります。ドラッグ＆ドロップで親子関係を自由に入れ替えることも可能です。

🕐 迷わず戻れる「最近開いた順モード」
ツリー構造だけでなく、最後に使った時刻順にタブを並べ替えることができます。たくさんのタブを開きっぱなしにしていても、今必要なタブにすぐアクセスできます。

📂 完璧な「グループ機能」連携
Chrome標準のタブグループに完全対応。サイドパネルからグループの色変更や名前の編集、一括操作がスムーズに行えます。

📌 固定タブ（ピン）専用セクション
大事なタブは上部に固定。通常のタブと切り分けて表示されるため、常に使いたいツールを見失いません。

💾 安心の「セッション保存」
現在のタブの状態（親子関係やピン留め、グループ状況まで！）をまるごと保存。後から別のウィンドウで一気に復元できるので、作業の切り替えも自由自在です。

🔍 履歴・ブックマークもこれひとつで
タブ管理だけでなく、履歴やブックマークの閲覧・検索機能も搭載。サイドパネルを開くだけで、ブラウザ内のあらゆる情報にアクセス可能です。

【その他のこだわり機能】
3つの表示モード: 通常・コンパクト・ミニから、あなたの画面サイズに最適なデザインを選べます。

タブ名のカスタム: わかりにくいページタイトルを自分好みの名前に書き換え可能。

ドメインまとめ: 同じドメインのタブをワンクリックで子タブとして集約。

ダークモード対応: 目に優しいデザインで、長時間の作業をサポート。

【使い方は簡単】
拡張機能をインストール。

ツールバーの拡張機能アイコンをクリックしてサイドパネルを開く。

左側のタブ一覧から、新しいブラウジング体験をスタート！

Bridge Productivity Booster powers up your new tab experience with a fresh way to start your journey. 

Save websites by just…

Bridge Productivity Booster powers up your new tab experience with a fresh way to start your journey. 

Save websites by just dragging them for quicker access, and group them as needed. Then, quickly open individual sites, entire groups as Chrome groups, or in new windows! 

Beyond websites, create tasks and notes. Plus, there's a built-in, fully customizable Pomodoro app to help you with your work. Create Modes to focus on one thing at a time, making only relevant items accessible when a mode is activated. 

Too many items? We've got you covered with global search—search everything in one place, or within the active mode. 

No mouse? Don't worry, there's a command palette too. Still not impressed? 

Personalize the app by changing its name, customizing the theme, and adding background images! 

This list is just getting started. Sign up now!!
OneLinks - A Tab Manager for Pros
OneLinks is a Chrome tab manager to save tab groups, organize sessions, search links, and restore workflows in one click.

OneLinks is a powerful tab manager and session manager for Chrome that helps you save tabs, organize browser sessions, and restore tab groups instantly. If you work with research tabs, project links, client resources, dashboards, and documentation, OneLinks keeps everything searchable and easy to reopen.

Key features:
- Save active tab groups in one click
- Organize saved tabs into clean link collections
- Restore entire browsing sessions with Open All
- Search tabs and links by title or URL
- Rename, move, copy, and delete links quickly
- Export tab collections as shareable HTML
- Import previously exported sessions
- Access OneLinks fast from the extension icon menu
- Keep your workspace available with pinned startup access

Why users install OneLinks:
- Reduce tab clutter and browser overload
- Improve productivity and focus
- Never lose important tabs again
- Manage multiple projects more efficiently
- Share curated link sets with teams or clients
Seamless sharing, multi-level bookmarking, AI-powered assistant, RAG source integration, tailored prompts & focus mode

🔗 Seamless IBM Integration for Enhanced Productivity!

Built for the IBM TechXchange event, this extension is your go-to tool for optimizing productivity and streamlining your experience with IBM Cloud. 🌐 Whether you're a developer, researcher, or power user, this extension offers a range of features to simplify your tasks and boost your efficiency.

Key Features:

📝 Tailored Prompts Storage: Save custom prompts and interact with AI-powered assistants to get precise responses directly within your browser.

🔖 Multi-Level Bookmarking: Bookmark key content across IBM’s cloud services, save code snippets, and highlight essential information with a few clicks.

📊 RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) Integration: Use advanced AI to search and fetch relevant documentation and answers from IBM resources.

🚫 Focus Mode: Block out distractions and limit your browsing only to IBM-related or cloud service pages, keeping you focused on your work.

💬 Contextual AI Assistant: Get instant insights, suggestions, and clarifications from WatsonX AI within the extension’s interface for a smarter workflow.

📁 Cloud Storage Integration: Securely save bookmarks, documents, and prompt history to access them anytime, from any device.

Why Install?

✨ Whether you're attending the IBM TechXchange event or just working with IBM Cloud, this extension will revolutionize your productivity. It offers a smooth user experience by making essential tools easily accessible right from your browser.

Start focusing on what really matters, save time, and let AI assist you along the way! 🚀

Private local workspace for sprint pages, clips, notes, and fast in-browser search.

Stop the tab hoarding. Start the research.

SprintTab is a privacy-first, local-only workspace designed for researchers, students, and power users who need to organize information without the clutter. Unlike traditional bookmark managers where links go to die, SprintTab treats your research as a "Sprint"—giving your saved pages a 7-day lifespan to keep you focused on what’s current.

Effortless "One-Click" Capture
Forget the friction of opening files or manually pasting text. With Alt+C, SprintTab instantly grabs your selected text and saves it directly into your clips. It happens in the background without any extra effort—no popups, no file-saving dialogs, just instant capture.

Synthesis Without the Tab-Switching
Stop flipping back and forth between tabs to compare information. Use Alt+H to summon your Quick History HUD. It displays your recent clips as floating cards directly on your current page. This allows you to view and compare text from different sources "on the spot" in the tab you are currently using.

Total Privacy
We believe your research is your business. SprintTab has no backend, no servers, and no tracking. All your data is stored locally in your browser’s IndexedDB and secure storage.

KEY FEATURES:

Direct Clipping (Alt+C): Save fragments instantly. It’s the fastest way to collect multiple short text clips from different websites and club them together into one organized workspace.

Comparison HUD (Alt+H): View your recent clips as transparent, floating cards. Compare data from Site A while you are still reading Site B—no more losing your place or switching tabs.

7-Day Sprint List: Save pages to a focused backlog. Items age visually over 7 days and auto-archive to keep your workspace fresh and relevant.

Command Center (Alt+S): Instantly open the SprintTab sidebar. This is your full-fledged workbench where all your clipped fragments, notes, and site links live together.

Sticky Notes (Alt+N): Drop digital sticky notes on any webpage to leave reminders or thoughts exactly where you need them.

Local Full-Text Search: Find any clip or saved page instantly. It’s 100% offline, incredibly fast, and keeps your data private.

WHY SPRINTTAB?

Stay in the Zone: Designed so you never have to break your concentration to organize your thoughts.

Club Your Research: Easily gather fragments from 10 different sites into one single, cohesive note.

No Account Required: Just install and start researching immediately.

Bigger, customizable tab tiles with groups, sizing controls, and fast navigation.

BoomerTabs is built for people who keep many tabs open and need a cleaner, faster way to navigate.

Key capabilities:

- Multi-row tab layouts with configurable row count
- Adjustable tabs-per-row, tab width, icon size, label size, and padding
- Drag-and-drop tab reordering directly in the custom rows
- Group-aware tabs with collapse support and clear group color markers
- Search and filter tabs by title or URL
- Search icon toggle that shows/hides a dedicated search row
- Footer or header placement
- Overlay mode and push mode (push avoids page overlap)
- Per-row horizontal scrolling (mouse wheel + trackpad gestures)
- Hidden scrollbar UI (keeps more vertical room for tabs/content)
- Pinned-tab handling with optional icon-only pinned tabs
- Quick hide/show controls and keyboard shortcuts
- Toolbar popup quick controls for layout/rows/groups without opening full settings
Search and open links from any webpage, fast and easy.
You can also exports links as an CSV file.
Dedicated-window command shell for tabs, windows, and groups.

BMXt is a UI demonstration for keyboard-centric browser operation on Chrome.
It exists because the author is not satisfied with today’s out-of-the-box browser experience.

It has a terminal-style prompt and log, but rather than being a classic terminal emulator alone, it is designed as a broad command surface for steering the whole browser—so you can keep your hands on the keyboard in a relaxed posture and cut down on how often you reach for tabs and windows.

Command output and typed command history are handled primarily in memory for the UI; only capped fields are persisted in chrome.storage.local. The extension page and service worker are not designed to call fetch() against arbitrary third-party HTTPS URLs (the packaged manifest’s CSP, including connect-src, reinforces this). Optional http(s) site access is requested at runtime only when you run commands that inject scripts into web pages.

◆About the name — a play on Browser Manipulator X Terminal:

- X
  - UX improvement
  - A hope that many different people can use it in crossover ways
  - Exponential growth and open-ended possibility through combining commands
- t
  - The first letter of Terminal
  - The idea of an extension layered onto your browser, carried in the letter t

The road has only just begun, but the author values your voices—so they decided to publish on the Chrome Web Store.
They believe the keyboard in front of you can take you “anywhere,” and will keep stacking features while building BMXt as a tool that stays human-centered.

◆Version history
0.4.1:
- Miscellaneous internal fixes
- Fixed a bug affecting text input in nav mode
- Removed the translation side picker; translate assist is now prompt preview and nav typing only

0.4.0:
- Fixed bugs in nav mode behavior
- Added translation assist (`translate -on` / `translate -off`)
- Adjusted Tab completion menu behavior
- Fixed welcome page open behavior

0.3.8:
- Show a welcome page on install and when the extension version is upgraded
- Fixed tabs mode filtering so keyboard focus no longer jumps to the list unexpectedly
- Revised mouse interaction: text in the prompt and log can be selected while keeping keyboard-first operation as the default
- Improved in-browser cross-search (`find`)
- Improved Tab completion menu behavior

0.3.5:
- Removed Rust to reduce package size
- Added the `:edit` subcommand in tabs mode for editing tab groups and window names
- Implemented IME-style input assistance while typing commands
- Moved list pickers for all commands to side columns beside the terminal and revised picker interaction (`Esc` returns to the prompt; close a column with `<cmd> -exit -list`)
- DOM list picker: focus rows with ↑/↓ and scroll the target page to the matching element
- Added nav mode: virtual cursor overlay on the active tab (`nav -enter`; Alt toggles ON/OFF; arrows move; Enter click/type; Ctrl context menu for text selection, image save, and reload)


0.2.1:
- Added the `dom` command so you can inspect the page source.
- Added the `find` command to search across internal data such as history, bookmarks, and open tabs.

0.1.1:
- Added the split command; add panes with split and remove them with exit (closes the window when the last pane exits)
- With multiple panes from split, Ctrl+Arrow moves focus between panes
- Clarified command behavior rules
- Added an assist menu while typing commands (type "tabs" and press Enter to try it)
- In tabs mode, "/" search: type a phrase and press Enter to highlight matches; press n to jump between them

0.0.9:
- Tab name search and highlight reset in tabs mode now follow Vim-style behavior (clear highlighted matches with :nohlsearch)
- Improved how release notes are specified and shown

◆Commands

| Command | Description |
|----------|------|
| `help` / `?` | Show help |
| `clear` | Clear logs |
| `exit` | Close the BMXt window and clear the session log |
| `split` | Show `-col` / `-row` options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `split ` (with a trailing space); `split -col` adds a pane beside the current one; `split -row` adds a pane below; with multiple panes, Ctrl+Arrow moves focus between panes |
| `tabs` | Show available options, then restore the prompt to `tabs ` (with a trailing space) for continued input |
| `tabs -list [-u]` | Open the tab picker; supports search, multi-select marker `#`, and bulk modes; `/` searches tab titles, Enter highlights matches, `n` jumps between them |
| `tabs -moveurl <url>` | Focus a tab with the given URL or open a new tab (http/https) |
| `tabs -nowurl` | Show the current tab’s URL |
| `dom` | Print available options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `dom ` (trailing space) for the next token |
| `dom -list [--html|--react] [<pattern>]` | Open a read-only DOM picker for the active tab (same picker chrome as `find -list`); flavor `--html` (default) or `--react`; optional `<pattern>` filters rendered lines with a case-insensitive substring (not a regex). ↑/↓ focus scrolls the target tab to the highlighted element. Scriptable http(s) pages only; may prompt for optional site access |
| `nav` | Print usage; Enter alone restores the prompt to `nav ` (trailing space) for `-enter` or `-exit` |
| `nav -enter` | Arm nav mode; **Alt** toggles the virtual cursor overlay on the target tab; arrows move; **Enter** click/type; **Ctrl** opens a context menu (text selection, save image, reload, history) |
| `nav -exit` | Fully disarm nav (turn the overlay **OFF** with **Alt** first) |
| `find` | Print available options; Enter alone restores the prompt to `find ` for a subcommand or flag |
| `find -list [--none|--history|--bookmark|--page] <pattern>` | Open a cross-search picker; default `--none` searches browsing history, bookmarks, and visible text in non-discarded http(s) tabs together; use one flag to limit scope. Case-insensitive substring match (no regex in v1) |
| `find --none <pattern>` | Search all three scopes in one go (log output; empty pattern returns capped “all” style hits) |
| `find --history <pattern>` | Search recent history titles/URLs only |
| `find --bookmark <pattern>` | Search bookmark titles/URLs only |
| `find --page <pattern>` | Search visible text in loaded http(s) tabs; may prompt for optional site access at runtime |
| `close` / `c <tabId>` | Close a tab |
| `group new <tabId> …` | Create a group |
| `notes` | Show release notes for the current extension version; `notes <version>` for a specific version (e.g. `0.1.1`); `notes --list` lists versions that have entries |
Organize, auto-group, search, save sessions, and surface stale tabs in your browser.

Tab Organizer keeps your browser Nice and Tidy.

Click the icon to pop open a clean, searchable list of every tab in your window — then sort, group, save, or close them in one click.

• Smart auto-grouping into 15 task categories: Development, Social, Shopping, Entertainment, News, Productivity, Research, AI Tools, Finance, Travel, Food, Gaming, Learning, Cloud, and Other. Hit Apply Groups to turn them into native, color-coded Chrome tab groups.

• Auto-group as you browse (optional). New tabs slot into the right group on their own; tabs you've already grouped manually are left alone.

• Named sessions. Save your current tabs as "Monday work" or "Vacation planning," restore them anytime, delete the ones you don't need.

• Stale-tab rescue. A red badge on the icon counts tabs you haven't visited in a while; one click saves them as a session and closes them.

• Custom rules. Add your own regex patterns and create your own categories with custom names, colors, and emoji.

• Close duplicates. One click kills every duplicate tab in the current window.

• Dark mode, synced across devices.

• Pixel buddies (just for fun). Eight tiny pixel-art creatures — slime, ghost, creeper, robot, cat, pumpkin, bee, skeleton — live at the bottom of every page. Drag them around. Hit Dance Mode and they bounce chaotically across the viewport. Add a per-site blocklist for places you don't want them.

Privacy: everything runs locally in your browser. No data is sent anywhere — your tabs, sessions, and rules stay on your device (synced across your own Chrome installs via Chrome's built-in sync).

Arc-style vertical tabs sidebar for Chrome. Organize tabs with drag & drop, groups, favorites, and a beautiful glassmorphism UI.

Arclight is the luxury upgrade for organized Chrome users. 

A stunning, forward-thinking sidebar helps you quickly see and organize tabs.  On-device AI auto-names your windows in realtime, and notices when a new tab should be in a different window.

Inspired by the Arc Browser, Arclight is the sidebar Chrome should have shipped with.

─────

WHAT YOU GET

 ◆ Light, Dark, and Auto themes — Gorgeous glassmorphism UI that matches your system
 ◆ Left or right sidebar positioning
 ◆ Smart window naming — Windows auto-name themselves based on your project
 ◆ Much more: Pinned tabs, favorite tabs, Chrome Tab Groups with color labels, rename and collapse tab lists, and more

─────

HOW IT WORKS

 ◆ Hover the edge of your screen to open the sidebar instantly
 ◆ Click any tab to switch to it, even across windows
 ◆ Drag tabs between windows or onto groups to organize
 ◆ Collapse windows and groups to focus on what matters
 ◆ Custom keyboard shortcut — Set your own hotkey to toggle the sidebar
 ◆ New in 1.1: Open a link from any external app — Arclight suggests the right window based on your workflow

─────

THOUGHTFUL DESIGN

 ◆ Your most recent window always rises to the top — no hunting for where you just were
 ◆ Tab titles are cleaned up automatically
 ◆ The active tab indicator only shows in the focused window
 ◆ Single-tab windows group at the bottom

─────

FREE DURING EARLY ACCESS

 ◆ Favorites — One-click bookmarks pinned to the top of your sidebar
 ◆ Pinned Tabs — Tabs that persist across browser restarts
 ◆ AI Window Names — On-device AI (Gemini Nano) generates smart window names based on your open tabs
 ◆ New in 1.1: Smart Tab Routing — When you open a link from an external app, on-device AI figures out which window it belongs in and offers to move it there with a single click

─────

PRIVACY FIRST

Arclight runs entirely locally. No data collected, no accounts, no cloud calls. The optional AI naming feature uses Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano model — everything stays on your device. Zero tracking, zero analytics, zero telemetry.
Hide All and Open in New Tab for HN. Only see the new stuff.

HackerNew improves a common use case for HackerNews readers. If, like me, you visit new.ycombinator.com regularly looking for interesting new stores to read then this extension is for you. 

Stories you want to read are opened in a new tab, contained in the HackerNew tab group. When you've pulled out all the stories of interest hit the hideAll button in the toolbar so that you get a fresh batch of stories the next time you visit.

Works great with the BrainTool bookmark organizer extension.

Click the icon, your tabs are saved. Organize with Crates, recover deleted tabs, and back them up to the cloud.

TabCrate is a fast, lightweight tab and session manager for Chrome. 
Save all your open tabs in one click, organize them by website automatically, and restore any session whenever you need it.

Never lose tabs again. TabCrate saves your tabs locally with one click (Alt+Shift+T) and optionally backs them up to the cloud. Chrome crashes or wipes your data, your tabs are still there.
FREE FEATURES

One-click save — click the icon or press Alt+Shift+T
Smart domain grouping — tabs auto-sort by website
Instant search — find any saved tab across all sessions
Crates — organize tabs into custom groups
Duplicate detection — never save the same tab twice
Lock & star groups — protect important sessions
Multi-select & drag-and-drop — reorganize tabs your way
Domain view — see all tabs from any site across every session
Import from OneTab, Toby, JSON, or plain text
Dark mode, no account required, no tracking

PRO — $3.49/MONTH

Encrypted cloud backup on every save (AES-256, end-to-end)
Cross-device sync — access saved tabs from any computer
Cross-profile sync — share tabs across Chrome profiles
Disaster recovery — restore tabs even after Chrome wipes local data
Unlimited tabs — no 500-tab soft limit

Your encryption password never leaves your device. We can't read your data.
